reforms.description: Ireland has increased the tax credit for incremental R&D expenditure from 20% to 25%, with new options to offset credits against past corporation taxes or receive cash where credits are exhausted. Additionally, the government plans to evaluate the cost-effectiveness of the system of tax credits for R&D, aiming to enhance the efficiency of public funding for research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in tax credits is expected to incentivize more R&D investment by firms, thereby enhancing innovation activity and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit increase has been put into effect, as indicated by the implementation status.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly alters the tax credit structure, encouraging increased R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: Tax credits for research and development (R&D) have been increased: the cost effectiveness of this support should be evaluated and direct support should be concentrated in fewer centres of world-class excellence.

reforms.description: Ireland introduced a Temporary Employment Subsidy Scheme (TESS) to provide financial support to firms in the manufacturing or traded services sector, aimed at retaining employees during economic downturns.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The scheme aims to stabilize employment in key sectors, which can help maintain productivity and support economic recovery.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme has been actively providing support to firms as indicated by its implementation status.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative provides significant support to firms but does not fundamentally change the structure of employment support.
reforms.source_quote: A Temporary Employment Subsidy Scheme (TESS) with an initial budget of €250million to provide a subsidy of up to €200 per week per worker to qualifying firms in the manufacturing or traded services sector to retain a person in employment for a period of up to 15 months.

reforms.description: The Back to Education Allowance was modified to make it easier for unemployed individuals to return to education while receiving benefits, aiming to improve their skills and employability.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to education, the reform aims to enhance the skill set of the workforce, which can lead to improved productivity and innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The modifications have been enacted and are currently in effect, as indicated by the implementation status.
reforms.importance_rationale: The modification improves access to education but does not represent a fundamental overhaul of the education system.
reforms.source_quote: The Back to Education Allowance has also been made easier to access and greater emphasis given to shorter education and training courses.

reforms.description: The government plans to invest up to €500 million through a new channel, Innovation Fund Ireland, linked to Enterprise Ireland for projects in R&D-intensive SMEs, supporting early-stage research and development. Additionally, the government intends to concentrate direct support for research and development in fewer world-class centres of excellence, which will improve coordination between researchers and industry.
reforms.package_name: Innovation Fund and R&D Support Strategy
reforms.component_name: Innovation Fund Ireland and Centre Concentration
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in R&D-intensive SMEs and the focus on centres of excellence are expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The plans for the Innovation Fund and concentration strategy have been publicly announced but not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a significant funding channel and strategic focus on excellence represents a major shift in R&D support.
reforms.source_quote: Plans to invest up to €500 million through a new channel, Innovation Fund Ireland, linked to Enterprise Ireland for projects in R&D-intensive SMEs.
